{{commons category|Category:Gomyo-Ten-jinja}} [[JA:天神社 (上里町五明)]] {{wikidata link|Q70725130}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenjin-sha |画像 = [[File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato).jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287}}Gomyō 871 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Tenjin-sha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin-sha''' (てんじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River}} in Gomyō, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It enshrines two deities, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami}}. It is a candidate shrine for "Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi Shrine" of Kami District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, in the Engishiki. Former shrine rank: village shrine. It was the guardian shrine of old Gomyō village. === Origin === The founding date is unknown. The shrine name is said to originate in rice-cultivation faith: immigrant-descended clans introduced advanced rice-growing techniques and enshrined the rice spiritCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Tenjin-sha Origin''. The immigrant-descended clans here refer to Imaki Kanjin, who came from Baekje on the Korean peninsula from the late 5th to early 6th century; because they migrated from their first settlement "Yamato no Kuni Imaki District," this is said to be the origin of the shrine-name element "Imaki." File:GomyouAma-jinja(Kamisato) Torii2.jpg|First torii File:Ama-jinja(Kamisato,Gomyou) Torii1.jpg|Second torii The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1722. In 1832, Iwamatsu Manjirō of Nitta District, Kōzuke Province, donated a black-crested curtain and tall lanterns to Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi DaimyōjinOrigin information board "Tenjin-sha" (installer: Kamisato Town, Saitama; installed March 1988). File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Haiden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall In 1967, a sacred stone rod inscribed: ''"emugi shikinai"'' ''"imaki aoyasaka no kami"'' was discovered in the precincts. This became the basis for treating the shrine as a candidate for the Engishiki shrine name. Today this stone rod is enshrined as the sacred object. '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) - village shrine * Taishō 8 (1919) Feb 3 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin shrine === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (わくむすびのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami}} (とようけひめのかみ) File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Stone monument === Precinct shrines === Merged in Meiji 10 (1877). * Wakamiya Hakusan-sha Merged in Meiji 42 (1909). {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287}}Gomyō 871 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Tenjin-sha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin-sha''' (てんじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River}} in Gomyō, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It enshrines two deities, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami}}. It is a candidate shrine for "Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi Shrine" of Kami District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, in the Engishiki. Former shrine rank: village shrine. It was the guardian shrine of old Gomyō village. === Origin === The founding date is unknown. The shrine name is said to originate in rice-cultivation faith: immigrant-descended clans introduced advanced rice-growing techniques and enshrined the rice spiritCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Tenjin-sha Origin''. The immigrant-descended clans here refer to Imaki Kanjin, who came from Baekje on the Korean peninsula from the late 5th to early 6th century; because they migrated from their first settlement "Yamato no Kuni Imaki District," this is said to be the origin of the shrine-name element "Imaki." File:GomyouAma-jinja(Kamisato) Torii2.jpg|First torii File:Ama-jinja(Kamisato,Gomyou) Torii1.jpg|Second torii The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1722. In 1832, Iwamatsu Manjirō of Nitta District, Kōzuke Province, donated a black-crested curtain and tall lanterns to Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi DaimyōjinOrigin information board "Tenjin-sha" (installer: Kamisato Town, Saitama; installed March 1988). File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Haiden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall In 1967, a sacred stone rod inscribed: ''"emugi shikinai"'' ''"imaki aoyasaka no kami"'' was discovered in the precincts. This became the basis for treating the shrine as a candidate for the Engishiki shrine name. Today this stone rod is enshrined as the sacred object. '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) - village shrine * Taishō 8 (1919) Feb 3 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin shrine === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (わくむすびのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami}} (とようけひめのかみ) File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Stone monument === Precinct shrines === Merged in Meiji 10 (1877). * Wakamiya Hakusan-sha Merged in Meiji 42 (1909). {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287}}Gomyō 871 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Tenjin-sha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin-sha''' (てんじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River}} in Gomyō, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It enshrines two deities, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami}}. It is a candidate shrine for "Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi Shrine" of Kami District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, in the Engishiki. Former shrine rank: village shrine. It was the guardian shrine of old Gomyō village. === Origin === The founding date is unknown. The shrine name is said to originate in rice-cultivation faith: immigrant-descended clans introduced advanced rice-growing techniques and enshrined the rice spiritCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Tenjin-sha Origin''. The immigrant-descended clans here refer to Imaki Kanjin, who came from Baekje on the Korean peninsula from the late 5th to early 6th century; because they migrated from their first settlement "Yamato no Kuni Imaki District," this is said to be the origin of the shrine-name element "Imaki." File:GomyouAma-jinja(Kamisato) Torii2.jpg|First torii File:Ama-jinja(Kamisato,Gomyou) Torii1.jpg|Second torii The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1722. In 1832, Iwamatsu Manjirō of Nitta District, Kōzuke Province, donated a black-crested curtain and tall lanterns to Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi DaimyōjinOrigin information board "Tenjin-sha" (installer: Kamisato Town, Saitama; installed March 1988). File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Haiden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall In 1967, a sacred stone rod inscribed: ''"emugi shikinai"'' ''"imaki aoyasaka no kami"'' was discovered in the precincts. This became the basis for treating the shrine as a candidate for the Engishiki shrine name. Today this stone rod is enshrined as the sacred object. '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) - village shrine * Taishō 8 (1919) Feb 3 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin shrine === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (わくむすびのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami}} (とようけひめのかみ) File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Stone monument === Precinct shrines === Merged in Meiji 10 (1877). * Wakamiya Hakusan-sha Merged in Meiji 42 (1909). {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287}}Gomyō 871 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Tenjin-sha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin-sha''' (てんじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River}} in Gomyō, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It enshrines two deities, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami}}. It is a candidate shrine for "Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi Shrine" of Kami District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, in the Engishiki. Former shrine rank: village shrine. It was the guardian shrine of old Gomyō village. === Origin === The founding date is unknown. The shrine name is said to originate in rice-cultivation faith: immigrant-descended clans introduced advanced rice-growing techniques and enshrined the rice spiritCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Tenjin-sha Origin''. The immigrant-descended clans here refer to Imaki Kanjin, who came from Baekje on the Korean peninsula from the late 5th to early 6th century; because they migrated from their first settlement "Yamato no Kuni Imaki District," this is said to be the origin of the shrine-name element "Imaki." File:GomyouAma-jinja(Kamisato) Torii2.jpg|First torii File:Ama-jinja(Kamisato,Gomyou) Torii1.jpg|Second torii The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1722. In 1832, Iwamatsu Manjirō of Nitta District, Kōzuke Province, donated a black-crested curtain and tall lanterns to Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi DaimyōjinOrigin information board "Tenjin-sha" (installer: Kamisato Town, Saitama; installed March 1988). File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Haiden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall In 1967, a sacred stone rod inscribed: ''"emugi shikinai"'' ''"imaki aoyasaka no kami"'' was discovered in the precincts. This became the basis for treating the shrine as a candidate for the Engishiki shrine name. Today this stone rod is enshrined as the sacred object. '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) - village shrine * Taishō 8 (1919) Feb 3 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin shrine === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (わくむすびのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami}} (とようけひめのかみ) File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Stone monument === Precinct shrines === Merged in Meiji 10 (1877). * Wakamiya Hakusan-sha Merged in Meiji 42 (1909). Located behind the shrine buildings. * Wakamiya Niu-sha (deity) Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto''Musashi no Kuni District/Village Records'' Vol.6, Hiraga District Gomyō Village (publisher: Saitama Prefecture, 1953) : Founded in March of {{ill|lt=Manju|Q1194155|WD=Q1194155}} 1 (1024) by Fujiwara Tomochika. Stone-hokora group on the right when facing the front of the haiden: * Suwa Shrine / Inari Shrine / Yasaka Shrine / Ichikishima Shrine File:GomyouTen-jinja(Kamisato) Tansho-sha.jpg|Niu-sha File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Stone hokora === Events === * New Year Festival (Jan 1) * Spring Festival (Mar 29) * Great Purification Rite (Jul 31) * Autumn Festival (Nov 19) * Niiname Festival (Dec 9) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133}}, take Kamisato town bus {{ill|lt=Komugicchi-go|Q28682946|WD=Q28682946}} (central route) bound for "Agri Park Kamisato" for 19 min. Get off at terminal "Agri Park Kamisato," then 15 min walk. * 45 min on foot (3.3 km) from JR Takasaki Line Jinbohara Station === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTenjin-sha (Q70725130)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTenjin-sha (Q70725130)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135039746|1=Imakiawoyasakainamino Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県児玉郡上里町五明871', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Gomyo-Ten-jinja}} [[JA:天神社 (上里町五明)]] {{wikidata link|Q70725130}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenjin-sha |画像 = [[File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato).jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}Gomyō 871 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Tenjin-sha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin-sha''' (てんじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Gomyō, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It enshrines two deities, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. It is a candidate shrine for "Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi Shrine" of Kami District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, in the Engishiki. Former shrine rank: village shrine. It was the guardian shrine of old Gomyō village. === Origin === The founding date is unknown. The shrine name is said to originate in rice-cultivation faith: immigrant-descended clans introduced advanced rice-growing techniques and enshrined the rice spiritCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Tenjin-sha Origin''. The immigrant-descended clans here refer to Imaki Kanjin, who came from Baekje on the Korean peninsula from the late 5th to early 6th century; because they migrated from their first settlement "Yamato no Kuni Imaki District," this is said to be the origin of the shrine-name element "Imaki." File:GomyouAma-jinja(Kamisato) Torii2.jpg|First torii File:Ama-jinja(Kamisato,Gomyou) Torii1.jpg|Second torii The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1722. In 1832, Iwamatsu Manjirō of Nitta District, Kōzuke Province, donated a black-crested curtain and tall lanterns to Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi DaimyōjinOrigin information board "Tenjin-sha" (installer: Kamisato Town, Saitama; installed March 1988). File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Haiden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall In 1967, a sacred stone rod inscribed: ''"emugi shikinai"'' ''"imaki aoyasaka no kami"'' was discovered in the precincts. This became the basis for treating the shrine as a candidate for the Engishiki shrine name. Today this stone rod is enshrined as the sacred object. '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) - village shrine * Taishō 8 (1919) Feb 3 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin shrine === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} (わくむすびのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (とようけひめのかみ) File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Stone monument === Precinct shrines === Merged in Meiji 10 (1877). * Wakamiya Hakusan-sha Merged in Meiji 42 (1909). Located behind the shrine buildings. * Wakamiya Niu-sha (deity) Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto''Musashi no Kuni District/Village Records'' Vol.6, Hiraga District Gomyō Village (publisher: Saitama Prefecture, 1953) : Founded in March of {{ill|lt=Manju|Q1194155|WD=Q1194155|1=Manju (era)}} 1 (1024) by Fujiwara Tomochika. [[File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato).jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}Gomyō 871 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Tenjin-sha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin-sha''' (てんじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Gomyō, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It enshrines two deities, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. It is a candidate shrine for "Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi Shrine" of Kami District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, in the Engishiki. Former shrine rank: village shrine. It was the guardian shrine of old Gomyō village. === Origin === The founding date is unknown. The shrine name is said to originate in rice-cultivation faith: immigrant-descended clans introduced advanced rice-growing techniques and enshrined the rice spiritCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Tenjin-sha Origin''. The immigrant-descended clans here refer to Imaki Kanjin, who came from Baekje on the Korean peninsula from the late 5th to early 6th century; because they migrated from their first settlement "Yamato no Kuni Imaki District," this is said to be the origin of the shrine-name element "Imaki." File:GomyouAma-jinja(Kamisato) Torii2.jpg|First torii File:Ama-jinja(Kamisato,Gomyou) Torii1.jpg|Second torii The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1722. In 1832, Iwamatsu Manjirō of Nitta District, Kōzuke Province, donated a black-crested curtain and tall lanterns to Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi DaimyōjinOrigin information board "Tenjin-sha" (installer: Kamisato Town, Saitama; installed March 1988). File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Haiden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall In 1967, a sacred stone rod inscribed: ''"emugi shikinai"'' ''"imaki aoyasaka no kami"'' was discovered in the precincts. This became the basis for treating the shrine as a candidate for the Engishiki shrine name. Today this stone rod is enshrined as the sacred object. '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) - village shrine * Taishō 8 (1919) Feb 3 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin shrine === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} (わくむすびのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (とようけひめのかみ) File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Stone monument === Precinct shrines === Merged in Meiji 10 (1877). * Wakamiya Hakusan-sha Merged in Meiji 42 (1909). Located behind the shrine buildings. * Wakamiya Niu-sha (deity) Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto''Musashi no Kuni District/Village Records'' Vol.6, Hiraga District Gomyō Village (publisher: Saitama Prefecture, 1953) : Founded in March of {{ill|lt=Manju|Q1194155|WD=Q1194155|1=Manju (era)}} 1 (1024) by Fujiwara Tomochika. Stone-hokora group on the right when facing the front of the haiden: * Suwa Shrine / Inari Shrine / Yasaka Shrine / Ichikishima Shrine File:GomyouTen-jinja(Kamisato) Tansho-sha.jpg|Niu-sha File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Stone hokora === Events === * New Year Festival (Jan 1) * Spring Festival (Mar 29) * Great Purification Rite (Jul 31) * Autumn Festival (Nov 19) * Niiname Festival (Dec 9) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}}, take Kamisato town bus {{ill|lt=Komugicchi-go|Q28682946|WD=Q28682946|1=Komugicchi-go}} (central route) bound for "Agri Park Kamisato" for 19 min. Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}Gomyō 871 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Tenjin-sha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin-sha''' (てんじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Gomyō, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It enshrines two deities, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. It is a candidate shrine for "Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi Shrine" of Kami District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, in the Engishiki. Former shrine rank: village shrine. It was the guardian shrine of old Gomyō village. === Origin === The founding date is unknown. The shrine name is said to originate in rice-cultivation faith: immigrant-descended clans introduced advanced rice-growing techniques and enshrined the rice spiritCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Tenjin-sha Origin''. The immigrant-descended clans here refer to Imaki Kanjin, who came from Baekje on the Korean peninsula from the late 5th to early 6th century; because they migrated from their first settlement "Yamato no Kuni Imaki District," this is said to be the origin of the shrine-name element "Imaki." File:GomyouAma-jinja(Kamisato) Torii2.jpg|First torii File:Ama-jinja(Kamisato,Gomyou) Torii1.jpg|Second torii The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1722. In 1832, Iwamatsu Manjirō of Nitta District, Kōzuke Province, donated a black-crested curtain and tall lanterns to Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi DaimyōjinOrigin information board "Tenjin-sha" (installer: Kamisato Town, Saitama; installed March 1988). File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Haiden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall In 1967, a sacred stone rod inscribed: ''"emugi shikinai"'' ''"imaki aoyasaka no kami"'' was discovered in the precincts. This became the basis for treating the shrine as a candidate for the Engishiki shrine name. Today this stone rod is enshrined as the sacred object. '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) - village shrine * Taishō 8 (1919) Feb 3 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin shrine === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} (わくむすびのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (とようけひめのかみ) File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Stone monument === Precinct shrines === Merged in Meiji 10 (1877). * Wakamiya Hakusan-sha Merged in Meiji 42 (1909). Located behind the shrine buildings. * Wakamiya Niu-sha (deity) Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto''Musashi no Kuni District/Village Records'' Vol.6, Hiraga District Gomyō Village (publisher: Saitama Prefecture, 1953) : Founded in March of {{ill|lt=Manju|Q1194155|WD=Q1194155|1=Manju (era)}} 1 (1024) by Fujiwara Tomochika. Stone-hokora group on the right when facing the front of the haiden: * Suwa Shrine / Inari Shrine / Yasaka Shrine / Ichikishima Shrine File:GomyouTen-jinja(Kamisato) Tansho-sha.jpg|Niu-sha File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Stone hokora === Events === * New Year Festival (Jan 1) * Spring Festival (Mar 29) * Great Purification Rite (Jul 31) * Autumn Festival (Nov 19) * Niiname Festival (Dec 9) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}}, take Kamisato town bus {{ill|lt=Komugicchi-go|Q28682946|WD=Q28682946|1=Komugicchi-go}} (central route) bound for "Agri Park Kamisato" for 19 min. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Tenjin-sha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin-sha''' (てんじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Gomyō, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It enshrines two deities, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. It is a candidate shrine for "Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi Shrine" of Kami District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, in the Engishiki. Former shrine rank: village shrine. It was the guardian shrine of old Gomyō village. === Origin === The founding date is unknown. The shrine name is said to originate in rice-cultivation faith: immigrant-descended clans introduced advanced rice-growing techniques and enshrined the rice spiritCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Tenjin-sha Origin''. The immigrant-descended clans here refer to Imaki Kanjin, who came from Baekje on the Korean peninsula from the late 5th to early 6th century; because they migrated from their first settlement "Yamato no Kuni Imaki District," this is said to be the origin of the shrine-name element "Imaki." File:GomyouAma-jinja(Kamisato) Torii2.jpg|First torii File:Ama-jinja(Kamisato,Gomyou) Torii1.jpg|Second torii The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1722. In 1832, Iwamatsu Manjirō of Nitta District, Kōzuke Province, donated a black-crested curtain and tall lanterns to Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi DaimyōjinOrigin information board "Tenjin-sha" (installer: Kamisato Town, Saitama; installed March 1988). File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Haiden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall In 1967, a sacred stone rod inscribed: ''"emugi shikinai"'' ''"imaki aoyasaka no kami"'' was discovered in the precincts. This became the basis for treating the shrine as a candidate for the Engishiki shrine name. Today this stone rod is enshrined as the sacred object. '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) - village shrine * Taishō 8 (1919) Feb 3 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin shrine === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} (わくむすびのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (とようけひめのかみ) File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Stone monument === Precinct shrines === Merged in Meiji 10 (1877). * Wakamiya Hakusan-sha Merged in Meiji 42 (1909). Located behind the shrine buildings. * Wakamiya Niu-sha (deity) Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto''Musashi no Kuni District/Village Records'' Vol.6, Hiraga District Gomyō Village (publisher: Saitama Prefecture, 1953) : Founded in March of {{ill|lt=Manju|Q1194155|WD=Q1194155|1=Manju (era)}} 1 (1024) by Fujiwara Tomochika. Stone-hokora group on the right when facing the front of the haiden: * Suwa Shrine / Inari Shrine / Yasaka Shrine / Ichikishima Shrine File:GomyouTen-jinja(Kamisato) Tansho-sha.jpg|Niu-sha File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Stone hokora === Events === * New Year Festival (Jan 1) * Spring Festival (Mar 29) * Great Purification Rite (Jul 31) * Autumn Festival (Nov 19) * Niiname Festival (Dec 9) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}}, take Kamisato town bus {{ill|lt=Komugicchi-go|Q28682946|WD=Q28682946|1=Komugicchi-go}} (central route) bound for "Agri Park Kamisato" for 19 min. Get off at terminal "Agri Park Kamisato," then 15 min walk. * 45 min on foot (3.3 km) from JR Takasaki Line Jinbohara Station === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QTenjin-sha (Q70725130)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTenjin-sha (Q70725130)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)}}[[Category:qq]]

{'text': '埼玉県児玉郡上里町五明871', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Gomyo-Ten-jinja}} [[JA:天神社 (上里町五明)]] {{wikidata link|Q70725130}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenjin-sha |画像 = [[File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato).jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}Gomyō 871 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Tenjin-sha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin-sha''' (てんじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Gomyō, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It enshrines two deities, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. It is a candidate shrine for "Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi Shrine" of Kami District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, in the Engishiki. Former shrine rank: village shrine. It was the guardian shrine of old Gomyō village. === Origin === The founding date is unknown. The shrine name is said to originate in rice-cultivation faith: immigrant-descended clans introduced advanced rice-growing techniques and enshrined the rice spiritCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Tenjin-sha Origin''. The immigrant-descended clans here refer to Imaki Kanjin, who came from Baekje on the Korean peninsula from the late 5th to early 6th century; because they migrated from their first settlement "Yamato no Kuni Imaki District," this is said to be the origin of the shrine-name element "Imaki." File:GomyouAma-jinja(Kamisato) Torii2.jpg|First torii File:Ama-jinja(Kamisato,Gomyou) Torii1.jpg|Second torii The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1722. In 1832, Iwamatsu Manjirō of Nitta District, Kōzuke Province, donated a black-crested curtain and tall lanterns to Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi DaimyōjinOrigin information board "Tenjin-sha" (installer: Kamisato Town, Saitama; installed March 1988). File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Haiden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall In 1967, a sacred stone rod inscribed: ''"emugi shikinai"'' ''"imaki aoyasaka no kami"'' was discovered in the precincts. This became the basis for treating the shrine as a candidate for the Engishiki shrine name. Today this stone rod is enshrined as the sacred object. Located behind the shrine buildings. * Wakamiya Niu-sha (deity) Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto''Musashi no Kuni District/Village Records'' Vol.6, Hiraga District Gomyō Village (publisher: Saitama Prefecture, 1953) : Founded in March of {{ill|lt=Manju|Q1194155|WD=Q1194155|1=Manju (era)}} 1 (1024) by Fujiwara Tomochika. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Tenjin-sha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin-sha''' (てんじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Gomyō, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It enshrines two deities, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. It is a candidate shrine for "Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi Shrine" of Kami District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, in the Engishiki. Former shrine rank: village shrine. It was the guardian shrine of old Gomyō village. === Origin === The founding date is unknown. The shrine name is said to originate in rice-cultivation faith: immigrant-descended clans introduced advanced rice-growing techniques and enshrined the rice spiritCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Tenjin-sha Origin''. The immigrant-descended clans here refer to Imaki Kanjin, who came from Baekje on the Korean peninsula from the late 5th to early 6th century; because they migrated from their first settlement "Yamato no Kuni Imaki District," this is said to be the origin of the shrine-name element "Imaki." File:GomyouAma-jinja(Kamisato) Torii2.jpg|First torii File:Ama-jinja(Kamisato,Gomyou) Torii1.jpg|Second torii The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1722. In 1832, Iwamatsu Manjirō of Nitta District, Kōzuke Province, donated a black-crested curtain and tall lanterns to Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi DaimyōjinOrigin information board "Tenjin-sha" (installer: Kamisato Town, Saitama; installed March 1988). File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Haiden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall In 1967, a sacred stone rod inscribed: ''"emugi shikinai"'' ''"imaki aoyasaka no kami"'' was discovered in the precincts. This became the basis for treating the shrine as a candidate for the Engishiki shrine name. Today this stone rod is enshrined as the sacred object. '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) - village shrine * Taishō 8 (1919) Feb 3 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin shrine === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} (わくむすびのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (とようけひめのかみ) File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Stone monument === Precinct shrines === Merged in Meiji 10 (1877). * Wakamiya Hakusan-sha Merged in Meiji 42 (1909). Located behind the shrine buildings. * Wakamiya Niu-sha (deity) Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto''Musashi no Kuni District/Village Records'' Vol.6, Hiraga District Gomyō Village (publisher: Saitama Prefecture, 1953) : Founded in March of {{ill|lt=Manju|Q1194155|WD=Q1194155|1=Manju (era)}} 1 (1024) by Fujiwara Tomochika. Stone-hokora group on the right when facing the front of the haiden: * Suwa Shrine / Inari Shrine / Yasaka Shrine / Ichikishima Shrine File:GomyouTen-jinja(Kamisato) Tansho-sha.jpg|Niu-sha File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Stone hokora === Events === * New Year Festival (Jan 1) * Spring Festival (Mar 29) * Great Purification Rite (Jul 31) * Autumn Festival (Nov 19) * Niiname Festival (Dec 9) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}}, take Kamisato town bus {{ill|lt=Komugicchi-go|Q28682946|WD=Q28682946|1=Komugicchi-go}} (central route) bound for "Agri Park Kamisato" for 19 min. Get off at terminal "Agri Park Kamisato," then 15 min walk. * 45 min on foot (3.3 km) from JR Takasaki Line Jinbohara Station === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]]

{'text': '埼玉県児玉郡上里町五明871', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Gomyo-Ten-jinja}} [[JA:天神社 (上里町五明)]] {{wikidata link|Q70725130}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenjin-sha |画像 = [[File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato).jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}Gomyō 871 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Tenjin-sha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin-sha''' (てんじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Gomyō, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It enshrines two deities, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. It is a candidate shrine for "Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi Shrine" of Kami District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, in the Engishiki. Former shrine rank: village shrine. It was the guardian shrine of old Gomyō village. === Origin === The founding date is unknown. The shrine name is said to originate in rice-cultivation faith: immigrant-descended clans introduced advanced rice-growing techniques and enshrined the rice spiritCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Tenjin-sha Origin''. The immigrant-descended clans here refer to Imaki Kanjin, who came from Baekje on the Korean peninsula from the late 5th to early 6th century; because they migrated from their first settlement "Yamato no Kuni Imaki District," this is said to be the origin of the shrine-name element "Imaki." File:GomyouAma-jinja(Kamisato) Torii2.jpg|First torii File:Ama-jinja(Kamisato,Gomyou) Torii1.jpg|Second torii The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1722. In 1832, Iwamatsu Manjirō of Nitta District, Kōzuke Province, donated a black-crested curtain and tall lanterns to Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi DaimyōjinOrigin information board "Tenjin-sha" (installer: Kamisato Town, Saitama; installed March 1988). File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Haiden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall In 1967, a sacred stone rod inscribed: ''"emugi shikinai"'' ''"imaki aoyasaka no kami"'' was discovered in the precincts. This became the basis for treating the shrine as a candidate for the Engishiki shrine name. Today this stone rod is enshrined as the sacred object. '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) - village shrine * Taishō 8 (1919) Feb 3 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin shrine === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} (わくむすびのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (とようけひめのかみ) File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Stone monument === Precinct shrines === Merged in Meiji 10 (1877). * Wakamiya Hakusan-sha Merged in Meiji 42 (1909). Located behind the shrine buildings. * Wakamiya Niu-sha (deity) Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto''Musashi no Kuni District/Village Records'' Vol.6, Hiraga District Gomyō Village (publisher: Saitama Prefecture, 1953) : Founded in March of {{ill|lt=Manju|Q1194155|WD=Q1194155|1=Manju (era)}} 1 (1024) by Fujiwara Tomochika. Stone-hokora group on the right when facing the front of the haiden: * Suwa Shrine / Inari Shrine / Yasaka Shrine / Ichikishima Shrine File:GomyouTen-jinja(Kamisato) Tansho-sha.jpg|Niu-sha File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Stone hokora === Events === * New Year Festival (Jan 1) * Spring Festival (Mar 29) * Great Purification Rite (Jul 31) * Autumn Festival (Nov 19) * Niiname Festival (Dec 9) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}}, take Kamisato town bus {{ill|lt=Komugicchi-go|Q28682946|WD=Q28682946|1=Komugicchi-go}} (central route) bound for "Agri Park Kamisato" for 19 min. Get off at terminal "Agri Park Kamisato," then 15 min walk. * 45 min on foot (3.3 km) from JR Takasaki Line Jinbohara Station === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]]

[[File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato).jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}Gomyō 871 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Tenjin-sha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin-sha''' (てんじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Gomyō, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It enshrines two deities, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. It is a candidate shrine for "Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi Shrine" of Kami District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, in the Engishiki. Former shrine rank: village shrine. It was the guardian shrine of old Gomyō village. === Origin === The founding date is unknown. The shrine name is said to originate in rice-cultivation faith: immigrant-descended clans introduced advanced rice-growing techniques and enshrined the rice spiritCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Tenjin-sha Origin''. The immigrant-descended clans here refer to Imaki Kanjin, who came from Baekje on the Korean peninsula from the late 5th to early 6th century; because they migrated from their first settlement "Yamato no Kuni Imaki District," this is said to be the origin of the shrine-name element "Imaki." File:GomyouAma-jinja(Kamisato) Torii2.jpg|First torii File:Ama-jinja(Kamisato,Gomyou) Torii1.jpg|Second torii The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1722. In 1832, Iwamatsu Manjirō of Nitta District, Kōzuke Province, donated a black-crested curtain and tall lanterns to Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi DaimyōjinOrigin information board "Tenjin-sha" (installer: Kamisato Town, Saitama; installed March 1988). File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Haiden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall In 1967, a sacred stone rod inscribed: ''"emugi shikinai"'' ''"imaki aoyasaka no kami"'' was discovered in the precincts. This became the basis for treating the shrine as a candidate for the Engishiki shrine name. Today this stone rod is enshrined as the sacred object. '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) - village shrine * Taishō 8 (1919) Feb 3 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin shrine === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} (わくむすびのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (とようけひめのかみ) File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Stone monument === Precinct shrines === Merged in Meiji 10 (1877). * Wakamiya Hakusan-sha Merged in Meiji 42 (1909). Located behind the shrine buildings. * Wakamiya Niu-sha (deity) Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto''Musashi no Kuni District/Village Records'' Vol.6, Hiraga District Gomyō Village (publisher: Saitama Prefecture, 1953) : Founded in March of {{ill|lt=Manju|Q1194155|WD=Q1194155|1=Manju (era)}} 1 (1024) by Fujiwara Tomochika. Stone-hokora group on the right when facing the front of the haiden: * Suwa Shrine / Inari Shrine / Yasaka Shrine / Ichikishima Shrine File:GomyouTen-jinja(Kamisato) Tansho-sha.jpg|Niu-sha File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Stone hokora === Events === * New Year Festival (Jan 1) * Spring Festival (Mar 29) * Great Purification Rite (Jul 31) * Autumn Festival (Nov 19) * Niiname Festival (Dec 9) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}}, take Kamisato town bus {{ill|lt=Komugicchi-go|Q28682946|WD=Q28682946|1=Komugicchi-go}} (central route) bound for "Agri Park Kamisato" for 19 min. Get off at terminal "Agri Park Kamisato," then 15 min walk. * 45 min on foot (3.3 km) from JR Takasaki Line Jinbohara Station === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]]

Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}Gomyō 871 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Tenjin-sha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin-sha''' (てんじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Gomyō, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It enshrines two deities, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. It is a candidate shrine for "Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi Shrine" of Kami District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, in the Engishiki. Former shrine rank: village shrine. It was the guardian shrine of old Gomyō village. === Origin === The founding date is unknown. The shrine name is said to originate in rice-cultivation faith: immigrant-descended clans introduced advanced rice-growing techniques and enshrined the rice spiritCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Tenjin-sha Origin''. The immigrant-descended clans here refer to Imaki Kanjin, who came from Baekje on the Korean peninsula from the late 5th to early 6th century; because they migrated from their first settlement "Yamato no Kuni Imaki District," this is said to be the origin of the shrine-name element "Imaki." File:GomyouAma-jinja(Kamisato) Torii2.jpg|First torii File:Ama-jinja(Kamisato,Gomyou) Torii1.jpg|Second torii The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1722. In 1832, Iwamatsu Manjirō of Nitta District, Kōzuke Province, donated a black-crested curtain and tall lanterns to Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi DaimyōjinOrigin information board "Tenjin-sha" (installer: Kamisato Town, Saitama; installed March 1988). File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Haiden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall In 1967, a sacred stone rod inscribed: ''"emugi shikinai"'' ''"imaki aoyasaka no kami"'' was discovered in the precincts. This became the basis for treating the shrine as a candidate for the Engishiki shrine name. Today this stone rod is enshrined as the sacred object. '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) - village shrine * Taishō 8 (1919) Feb 3 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin shrine === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} (わくむすびのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (とようけひめのかみ) File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Stone monument === Precinct shrines === Merged in Meiji 10 (1877). * Wakamiya Hakusan-sha Merged in Meiji 42 (1909). Located behind the shrine buildings. * Wakamiya Niu-sha (deity) Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto''Musashi no Kuni District/Village Records'' Vol.6, Hiraga District Gomyō Village (publisher: Saitama Prefecture, 1953) : Founded in March of {{ill|lt=Manju|Q1194155|WD=Q1194155|1=Manju (era)}} 1 (1024) by Fujiwara Tomochika. Stone-hokora group on the right when facing the front of the haiden: * Suwa Shrine / Inari Shrine / Yasaka Shrine / Ichikishima Shrine File:GomyouTen-jinja(Kamisato) Tansho-sha.jpg|Niu-sha File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Stone hokora === Events === * New Year Festival (Jan 1) * Spring Festival (Mar 29) * Great Purification Rite (Jul 31) * Autumn Festival (Nov 19) * Niiname Festival (Dec 9) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}}, take Kamisato town bus {{ill|lt=Komugicchi-go|Q28682946|WD=Q28682946|1=Komugicchi-go}} (central route) bound for "Agri Park Kamisato" for 19 min. former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Tenjin-sha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin-sha''' (てんじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Gomyō, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It enshrines two deities, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. It is a candidate shrine for "Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi Shrine" of Kami District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, in the Engishiki. Former shrine rank: village shrine. It was the guardian shrine of old Gomyō village. === Origin === The founding date is unknown. The shrine name is said to originate in rice-cultivation faith: immigrant-descended clans introduced advanced rice-growing techniques and enshrined the rice spiritCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Tenjin-sha Origin''. The immigrant-descended clans here refer to Imaki Kanjin, who came from Baekje on the Korean peninsula from the late 5th to early 6th century; because they migrated from their first settlement "Yamato no Kuni Imaki District," this is said to be the origin of the shrine-name element "Imaki." File:GomyouAma-jinja(Kamisato) Torii2.jpg|First torii File:Ama-jinja(Kamisato,Gomyou) Torii1.jpg|Second torii The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1722. In 1832, Iwamatsu Manjirō of Nitta District, Kōzuke Province, donated a black-crested curtain and tall lanterns to Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi DaimyōjinOrigin information board "Tenjin-sha" (installer: Kamisato Town, Saitama; installed March 1988). File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Haiden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall In 1967, a sacred stone rod inscribed: ''"emugi shikinai"'' ''"imaki aoyasaka no kami"'' was discovered in the precincts. This became the basis for treating the shrine as a candidate for the Engishiki shrine name. Today this stone rod is enshrined as the sacred object. '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) - village shrine * Taishō 8 (1919) Feb 3 - shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin shrine === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} (わくむすびのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (とようけひめのかみ) File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Stone monument === Precinct shrines === Merged in Meiji 10 (1877). * Wakamiya Hakusan-sha Merged in Meiji 42 (1909). Located behind the shrine buildings. * Wakamiya Niu-sha (deity) Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto''Musashi no Kuni District/Village Records'' Vol.6, Hiraga District Gomyō Village (publisher: Saitama Prefecture, 1953) : Founded in March of {{ill|lt=Manju|Q1194155|WD=Q1194155|1=Manju (era)}} 1 (1024) by Fujiwara Tomochika. Stone-hokora group on the right when facing the front of the haiden: * Suwa Shrine / Inari Shrine / Yasaka Shrine / Ichikishima Shrine File:GomyouTen-jinja(Kamisato) Tansho-sha.jpg|Niu-sha File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Stone hokora === Events === * New Year Festival (Jan 1) * Spring Festival (Mar 29) * Great Purification Rite (Jul 31) * Autumn Festival (Nov 19) * Niiname Festival (Dec 9) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}{{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|WD=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}}, take Kamisato town bus {{ill|lt=Komugicchi-go|Q28682946|WD=Q28682946|1=Komugicchi-go}} (central route) bound for "Agri Park Kamisato" for 19 min. Get off at terminal "Agri Park Kamisato," then 15 min walk. * 45 min on foot (3.3 km) from JR Takasaki Line Jinbohara Station === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin-sha (Q70725130)]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]]

content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenjin-sha |画像 = [[File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato).jpg|270px]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}Gomyō 871 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Tenjin-sha|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin-sha''' (てんじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kanna River (Tone system)|Q11590491|WD=Q11590491|lt=Kanna River|1=Kanna River}} in Gomyō, {{ill|lt=Kamisato|Q1345287|WD=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It enshrines two deities, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}} and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}}. It is a candidate shrine for "Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi Shrine" of Kami District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, in the Engishiki. Former shrine rank: village shrine. It was the guardian shrine of old Gomyō village. === Origin === The founding date is unknown. The shrine name is said to originate in rice-cultivation faith: immigrant-descended clans introduced advanced rice-growing techniques and enshrined the rice spiritCommemorative booklet for the 62nd Ise Grand Shrine rebuilding, ''Tenjin-sha Origin''. The immigrant-descended clans here refer to Imaki Kanjin, who came from Baekje on the Korean peninsula from the late 5th to early 6th century; because they migrated from their first settlement "Yamato no Kuni Imaki District," this is said to be the origin of the shrine-name element "Imaki." File:GomyouAma-jinja(Kamisato) Torii2.jpg|First torii File:Ama-jinja(Kamisato,Gomyou) Torii1.jpg|Second torii The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in 1722. In 1832, Iwamatsu Manjirō of Nitta District, Kōzuke Province, donated a black-crested curtain and tall lanterns to Imaki Aoyasaka Inemi DaimyōjinOrigin information board "Tenjin-sha" (installer: Kamisato Town, Saitama; installed March 1988). File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Haiden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:GomyouAma-jinja(Misato) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall In 1967, a sacred stone rod inscribed: ''"emugi shikinai"'' ''"imaki aoyasaka no kami"'' was discovered in the precincts. This became the basis for treating the shrine as a candidate for the Engishiki shrine name. Today this stone rod is enshrined as the sacred object.
